libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not attempt to restore or test org.apache.pekko.actor.TypedActor / TypedProps APIs: they are present in 1.1.5 but absent from the resolved 2.0.0-M1 actor jar. Meaningful tests should use supported classic actor APIs such as ActorSystem, Props, ActorRef, ConfigFactory-based configuration, serialization, dispatchers, and lifecycle/termination handling. If a test directly touches JDK-internal DNS APIs through Pekko DNS settings, keep the existing --add-exports=java.base/sun.net.dns=ALL-UNNAMED style setup; otherwise no special JVM setup is needed.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 1.1.5 tests that reference TypedActor APIs will fail to compile against 2.0.0-M1 because those classes are no longer in the actor artifact.

Pekko documentation commonly uses pekko-testkit for unit testing actors, but it is not required to exercise pekko-actor itself; adding it preemptively could shift coverage toward a separate test artifact.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is concretely required before meaningful coverage of org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1 is reachable. The artifact resolves with only scala-library and com.typesafe:config as runtime dependencies, and normal actor usage is in-process via ActorSystem, Props, actors, dispatchers, and configuration.
